## Part 3 — REWRITE the `lem:cech_augmented_resolution` PROOF sketch to the sections/sheafification route

**This is the most important part.** The current proof block (around lines 7029–7066) sketches a
stalk-at-prime argument. That route requires a `SheafOfModules.stalk` functor and an "exact iff stalkwise
exact" criterion that are BOTH absent from Mathlib. Replace the proof body with the following route

`X.Modules` is proved by showing each homology object `Hᵖ` (`p ≥ 1`, plus the augmentation node) is zero,
via:
1. **Reflect through the faithful `toSheaf`.** `IsZero (Hᵖ in X.Modules)` follows from `IsZero` of its
   image under `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (to abelian sheaves), because `toSheaf` is faithful and additive:
   `CategoryTheory.Functor.reflects_exact_of_faithful` needs only `[Faithful]` + `[PreservesZeroMorphisms]`,
   both Mathlib instances on `toSheaf` (no `PreservesFiniteColimits` needed).
2. **Homology sheaf = sheafification of the presheaf homology.** By `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`
   (project, `HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean`), the degree-`p` homology of the sheaf complex is the
   sheafification of the degree-`p` homology of the underlying PRESHEAF complex. The presheaf homology at
   an open `V` is the homology of the section complex `C•(V)` (= the Čech cochain complex of `F` over the
   cover restricted to `V`).
3. **The presheaf homology is locally zero on the affine basis.** This presheaf is nonzero in general (it
   is `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V,·)`), but it VANISHES locally: over any basic affine `D(g)` small enough to sit inside a
   cover element, the section-Čech complex of `~M` is exact in positive degrees — exactly

   `0 → (presheaf homology)` is a local isomorphism (`J.W`, a `LocallyBijective` W-equivalence), so its
   sheafification is an iso: `sheafify(presheaf homology) ≅ 0`. (Mathlib: `Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`,
   `Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_presheafToSheaf_map_iff`, `GrothendieckTopology.WEqualsLocallyBijective`
   for `AddCommGrp`; the cofinal affine basis is `standard_cover_cofinal` / `affineCoverSystem`.)
4. **Augmentation node (degree 0).** Separately, `ε : F → C⁰` is the kernel inclusion: `H⁰` of the
   augmented complex is zero because over each affine the augmented section complex is exact at degree 0
   (`exact_of_isLocalized_span`, complementing the degree ≥1 vanishing).

205	## Mathlib dependency anchors (`\mathlibok`)
206	
207	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:
208	
209	- makes the reliance explicit and readable (a mathematician sees exactly which Mathlib result the route stands on);
210	- gives `leandag` a real node, so the `\uses{}` resolves (no broken/unknown ref) and the node's effort is **done** rather than ∞;
211	- keeps it clear how much the route leans on Mathlib vs. on original work.
212	
213	Write the anchor like any statement block, with three differences:
214	
215	```latex
216	\begin{lemma}[Hensel's lemma]
217	  \label{lem:hensel_mathlib}
218	  \lean{Mathlib.RingTheory.Henselian.foo}   % the REAL Mathlib declaration
219	  \mathlibok
220	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
221	  Statement of the result in the project's notation.
222	\end{lemma}
223	```
224	
225	- **`\lean{}` names the actual Mathlib declaration** (with its real namespace), so `leandag` matches the node to Mathlib.
226	- **`\mathlibok`** marks it as supplied by Mathlib — the DAG treats it as done; no `\begin{proof}` is needed.
227	- No `% SOURCE:`/`% SOURCE QUOTE:` block — Mathlib is the source; the `\lean{}` target *is* the citation.
228	
229	**The anti-hallucination rule (mirrors citation discipline):** mark `\mathlibok` ONLY when the statement genuinely exists in Mathlib and your stated form is faithful to it. A wrong or invented `\mathlibok` is worse than an ∞ hole — the loop will treat a non-existent result as available and skip proving a real gap. So:
230	
231	- Prefer anchors the **directive names** (the plan agent/reviewer identified them as Mathlib results).
232	- If you are not confident the exact statement is in Mathlib, do **not** mark `\mathlibok` — write the block without it (or leave the `\uses{}` for the reviewer to resolve) and flag it under "Notes for Plan Agent" so the reviewer can verify against Lean.
233	- Never put `\mathlibok` on one of the project's **own** to-be-proved declarations. That marker is for Mathlib-supplied results only; `\leanok` (project proofs) is never yours to add.

6328	  i_p})\). These assemble into a cosimplicial morphism (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial
6329	  map}), functorial in \(\varphi\) (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial functor}). Composing
6330	  with the alternating-coface-map complex functor packages the section {\v C}ech complex as a
6331	  functor in the coefficient presheaf (the \emph{section {\v C}ech complex functor}) and yields,
6332	  for each \(\varphi\), the induced chain map of section {\v C}ech complexes
6333	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \varphi)\). These are the maps used to build the short
6334	  complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex}.
6335	\end{definition}
6336	
6337	\begin{definition}
6338	
6339	\leanok
6340	[Per-face section short complex]
6341	  \label{def:face_short_complex}
6342	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.faceShortComplex}
6343	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
6344	  \textit{Project-local building block.}
6345	  Let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family and let
6346	  \(P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3\) be a short complex of presheaves of
6347	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. For a degree \(p\) and a tuple \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\)
6348	  write \(V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\) for the corresponding basic open. The \emph{per-face
6349	  short complex} \(\mathrm{face}(U, P, \sigma)\) is the short complex of abelian groups obtained by
6350	  evaluating \(P\) on sections over \(V_\sigma\),
6351	  \[
6352	    \mathcal{P}_1(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow \mathcal{P}_2(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow
6353	      \mathcal{P}_3(V_\sigma),
6354	  \]
6355	  the maps being the section-level actions over \(V_\sigma\) of the two maps of \(P\). It is the
6356	  \((p, \sigma)\)-indexed factor whose product over all tuples \(\sigma\) is the degree-\(p\) term
6357	  of the section {\v C}ech complex.
6358	\end{definition}
6359	
6360	\begin{definition}
6361	
6362	\leanok
6363	[Short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes]
6364	  \label{def:section_cech_short_complex}
6365	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexShortComplex}
6366	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:section_cech_functoriality}
6367	  \textit{Project-local.}
6368	  For an index family \(U\) and a short complex
6369	  \(P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3\) of presheaves of
6370	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the \emph{short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes} is
6371	  \[
6372	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_1) \to
6373	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_2) \to
6374	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_3) \to 0,
6375	  \]
6376	  a short complex in the category of cochain complexes of abelian groups, with the two maps the
6377	  chain maps induced by the maps of \(P\) through the functoriality of
6378	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_functoriality}. Its short-exactness is the conclusion of
6379	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis} and the input to Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}.
6380	\end{definition}
6381	
6382	\begin{definition}
6383	
6384	[Cover system on a basis]
6385	  \label{def:basis_cov_system}
6386	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.BasisCovSystem, AlgebraicGeometry.CovDatum}
6387	  \uses{def:cech_complex, lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:injective_cech_acyclic}
6388	  \textit{Project-bespoke encoding of the hypotheses of Stacks 01EO.}
6389	  A \emph{cover system on a basis} for a scheme \(X\) is a record with five fields. A
6390	  \emph{covering datum} (the helper \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\)) is a pair \((\iota, U)\) of an index type
6391	  \(\iota\) together with an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\); it is the raw shape
6392	  \(\Sigma\,\iota,\ \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) over which the section {\v C}ech complex is built.
6393	  The cover system bundles:
6394	  \begin{enumerate}
6395	  \item a basis \(\mathcal{B} \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) for the topology;
6396	  \item a set \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of admissible coverings, each a \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\);
6397	  \item \emph{faces-in-basis} (\(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\)): for every covering in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) every
6398	    finite intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) of its opens lies in \(\mathcal{B}\) (condition (1) of
6399	    Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis});
6400	  \item \emph{section surjectivity} (\(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\)): for every \(V \in \mathcal{B}\)
6401	    and every short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to S_3 \to 0\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
6402	    whose left term \(S_1\) has vanishing positive {\v C}ech cohomology over the coverings of \(V\) in
6403	    \(\mathrm{Cov}\), the section map \(S_2(V) \to S_3(V)\) is surjective;
6404	  \item \emph{injective acyclicity} (\(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\)): for every injective
6405	    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) and every covering \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) the
6406	    positive-degree {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes, \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for
6407	    all \(p > 0\).
6408	  \end{enumerate}
6409	  Unlike a bare combinatorial record, the structure carries two sheaf-theoretic ({\v C}ech-cohomology)
6410	  fields. The field \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) is \emph{not} the raw cofinality datum: it is the
6411	  section-surjectivity \emph{output} that cofinality together with Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}
6412	  produces, packaged directly as the consequence the 01EO chain consumes. The field
6413	  \(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\) is the injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity of
6414	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} for the system's own coverings. These are exactly the data
6415	  that the dimension-shift induction Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} consumes.
6416	\end{definition}
6417	
6418	\begin{definition}
6419	
6420	\leanok
6421	[Vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for a cover system]
6422	  \label{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}
6423	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.HasVanishingHigherCech}
6424	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
6425	  \textit{Project-bespoke inductive predicate (condition (3) of Stacks 01EO, per module).}
6426	  Given a cover system \(s\) on a basis (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system}), an
6427	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for
6428	  \(s\)} when
6429	  \[
6430	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad
6431	      \text{for every } \mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov} \text{ and every } p > 0,
6432	  \]
6433	  with \(\check{H}^p\) the accessor of Definition~\ref{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}. This is the
6434	  per-module form of condition (3). It is deliberately stated for an \emph{arbitrary}
6435	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module: the dimension-shift induction of
6436	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} feeds the quotient \(\mathcal{Q} =
6437	  \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\) back as the coefficient, and \(\mathcal{Q}\) need not be quasi-coherent
6438	  even when \(\mathcal{F}\) is. The inductive class is therefore ``\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules with
6439	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)'', a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) entering only
6440	  as the seed at the affine (Tag 02KG) instantiation; the predicate must \emph{not} be specialized
6441	  to quasi-coherent modules.
6442	\end{definition}
6443	
6444	\begin{lemma}

6960	  The argument never uses affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing
6961	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is condition (3),
6962	  which on the affine instantiation is supplied by the standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
6963	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. Thus the dependency
6964	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the graph is
6965	  acyclic.
6966	\end{proof}
6967	
6968	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
6969	
6970	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
6971	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
6972	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
6973	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
6974	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
6975	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
6976	
6977	\begin{lemma}
6978	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
6979	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
6980	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
6981	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
6982	  % NOTE (iter-051): the augmented-complex OBJECT layer is now built axiom-clean in
6983	  % CechHigherDirectImage.lean (cechComplexOnX, cechNervePointIso, cechAugmentation,
6984	  % cechAugmentation_comp_d, cechAugmentedComplex). The `\lean{}` target `cechAugmented_exact`
6985	  % (the EXACTNESS theorem) is NOT yet built: its stalk-at-prime proof needs a
6986	  % "complex of sheaves of modules is exact iff exact on every stalk" criterion for X.Modules
6987	  % that is ABSENT from Mathlib (no SheafOfModules.stalk; no stalkwise-exactness reflection).
6988	  % Planner: consider splitting this lemma into "object (built)" + "exactness (pending)", and
6989	  % blueprint the missing stalkwise-exactness criterion as its own sub-lemma chain
6990	  % (see task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.md decomposition steps 1-2).
6991	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
6992	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
6993	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
6994	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
6995	  % \begin{equation}
6996	  % \label{equation-extended}
6997	  % 0 \to
6998	  % M \to
6999	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
7000	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
7001	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
7002	  % \ldots
7003	  % \end{equation}
7004	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
7005	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
7006	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
7007	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
7008	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
7009	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7010	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7011	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
7012	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
7013	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
7014	  \[
7015	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
7016	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
7017	    \qquad
7018	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
7019	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
7020	  \]
7021	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
7022	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
7023	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
7024	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
7025	\end{lemma}
7026	
7027	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
7028	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
7029	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
7030	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
7031	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
7032	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
7033	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. Note that
7034	% $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$. ...
7035	% Let us define a homotopy ... by the rule
7036	% $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$"
7037	% (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L80--110).
7038	\begin{proof}
7039	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections, lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}
7040	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7041	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7042	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is a \emph{local}
7043	  question: the complex is exact if and only if it is exact on every stalk
7044	  (equivalently, after localizing the sections at every prime). It therefore
7045	  suffices to check exactness of the augmented {\v C}ech complex stalkwise.
7046	
7047	  Fix an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) of \(X\) and a prime
7048	  \(\mathfrak{p} \in \operatorname{Spec}(A)\). By the affine tilde isomorphism
7049	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} and \ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}) we have
7050	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U \cong \widetilde{M}\) with \(M = \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\), and the
7051	  sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over the faces are the away-localizations
7052	  \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the augmented {\v C}ech
7053	  complex over \(U\) is thus the localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the extended
7054	  complex of localizations
7055	  \[
7056	    0 \;\to\; M \;\to\; \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
7057	      \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\; \cdots.
7058	  \]
7059	  Since the \(\{f_i\}\) cover \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), for the prime
7060	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) there is an index \(i\) with \(f_i \notin \mathfrak{p}\); that is,
7061	  \(f_i\) becomes a \emph{unit} in \(A_{\mathfrak{p}}\). Fixing one such index
7062	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), the standard ``one index is a unit'' contracting homotopy
7063	  \[
7064	    h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \;=\; s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, i_0 \ldots i_p}
7065	  \]
7066	  witnesses that the localized extended complex is homotopic to zero, hence exact in
7067	  every positive degree and at the augmentation. This is precisely the standard-cover
7068	  {\v C}ech vanishing already established at the level of sections in
7069	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
7070	
7071	  As this holds for every prime \(\mathfrak{p}\) — hence on every stalk — the
7072	  augmented {\v C}ech complex is exact on \(X\); that is, the {\v C}ech nerve of
7073	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
7074	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
7075	\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact, lem:section_cech_homology_exact,
    lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway, lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule}
  Set \(R_f = \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\) and \(M_f =
  \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} f)\, M\). Following the Stacks proof's
  ``Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\)'' step, we run the whole computation over the ring \(R_f\),
  where the cover \emph{does} span the unit ideal, and transport the resulting exactness back to \(R\)
  algebraically.

  \emph{The \(R_f\)-side complex is exact.} The un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex
  \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} is polymorphic in the base ring:
  for any commutative ring, any module over it, and any spanning family of the unit ideal, its
  positive-degree exactness is Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}. Instantiate that lemma over
  \(R_f\) with the module \(M_f\) and the family \(g/1 : \iota \to R_f\),
  \((g/1)_i = \operatorname{algebraMap} R\, R_f\, (g_i)\). Over \(R_f\) this family spans the unit
  ideal (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway}, using \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\)), so
  the spanning hypothesis of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} is met and the \(R_f\)-side
  complex \(D^\bullet_{R_f}\) --- whose degree-\(p\) term is \(\prod_\sigma (M_f)_{(g/1)_\sigma}\) ---
  is exact in positive degrees.

  \emph{Degreewise transfer to the \(R\)-side.} For a tuple \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) put
  \(g_\sigma = \prod_k g_{\sigma k}\). Since each \(D(g_i) \subseteq D(f)\) we have \(D(g_\sigma)
  \subseteq D(f)\), hence \(g_\sigma \in \sqrt{(f)}\) and \(f\) is invertible in the away localisation
  \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The transitivity comparison of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule} therefore exhibits a canonical isomorphism of
  abelian groups
  \[
    M_{g_\sigma} \;\cong\; (M_f)_{g_\sigma},
  \]
  both sides presenting \(M\) localised away from \(g_\sigma f\). These per-tuple isomorphisms are the
  vertical maps of a ladder between the \(R\)-side section {\v C}ech module complex of \(\widetilde{M}\)
  over \(\{D(g_i)\}\) and the \(R_f\)-side complex \(D^\bullet_{R_f}\). They commute with the
  alternating-sum localisation differentials, because each {\v C}ech coface is itself an
  away-localisation comparison map and the comparisons of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule} are natural in the localisation base (the same
  naturality square intertwining the complex differential with the section cofaces in
  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}). Transporting positive-degree exactness across this
  additive ladder --- an exactness transfer along an isomorphism of complexes --- yields positive-degree
  exactness for the \(R\)-side section {\v C}ech differentials.

  \emph{From exactness to vanishing homology.} Wrapping positive-degree function exactness as the
  vanishing of the degree-\(p\) homology --- exactly as Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
  does for the whole-space case --- gives \(\check{H}^p(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}) = 0\) for all
  \(p > 0\).
\end{proof}
input.new_string:
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact, lem:section_cech_homology_exact,
    lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway, lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule,
    lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_localizationAway, lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away}
  Set \(R_f = \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\). Following the Stacks proof's ``Write
  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\)'' step, we run the whole computation over the ring \(R_f\), where the
  cover \emph{does} span the unit ideal. The two algebraic inputs this needs are read off from the
  geometry of the cover.

  \emph{(a) Each \(g_i\) lies in the radical of \((f)\).} From \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) we have in
  particular \(D(g_i) \subseteq D(f)\) for every \(i\), and the inclusion of basic opens
  \(D(g_i) \subseteq D(f)\) is equivalent to \(g_i \in \sqrt{(f)}\) (a power of \(g_i\) is a multiple of
  \(f\)). \emph{(b) The cover spans over \(R_f\).} Because \(f\) is a unit in \(R_f\), the equality
  \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) pulls back to \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i/1)\), so the
  images \(g_i/1\) span the unit ideal of \(R_f\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway}).

  With (a) and (b) in hand, Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_localizationAway} supplies the
  positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet(g, M)\):
  internally it instantiates the polymorphic exactness of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} over \(R_f\) with the module
  \(M_f = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} f)\, M\), and transports the
  result back along the degreewise isomorphisms \(M_{g_\sigma} \cong (M_f)_{g_\sigma}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away} (for a tuple \(\sigma\), \(g_\sigma = \prod_k g_{\sigma k}
  \in \sqrt{(f)}\), so both sides present \(M\) localised away from \(g_\sigma f\)).

  Finally, the tilde-bridge ladder identifies the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\widetilde{M}\) over
  \(\{D(g_i)\}\) with this localised-module complex --- via the same section-to-module identification
  and coface matching used in Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} --- and wraps positive-degree
  exactness as the vanishing of the degree-\(p\) homology, giving
  \(\check{H}^p(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}

  Exactness of a complex of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is a \emph{local}
  question: the complex is exact if and only if it is exact on every stalk
  (equivalently, after localizing the sections at every prime). It therefore
  suffices to check exactness of the augmented {\v C}ech complex stalkwise.

  Fix an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) of \(X\) and a prime
  \(\mathfrak{p} \in \operatorname{Spec}(A)\). By the affine tilde isomorphism
  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} and \ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}) we have
  \(\mathcal{F}|_U \cong \widetilde{M}\) with \(M = \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\), and the
  sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over the faces are the away-localizations
  \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the augmented {\v C}ech
  complex over \(U\) is thus the localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the extended
  complex of localizations
  \[
    0 \;\to\; M \;\to\; \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
      \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\; \cdots.
  \]
  Since the \(\{f_i\}\) cover \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), for the prime
  \(\mathfrak{p}\) there is an index \(i\) with \(f_i \notin \mathfrak{p}\); that is,
  \(f_i\) becomes a \emph{unit} in \(A_{\mathfrak{p}}\). Fixing one such index
  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), the standard ``one index is a unit'' contracting homotopy
  \[
    h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \;=\; s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, i_0 \ldots i_p}
  \]
  witnesses that the localized extended complex is homotopic to zero, hence exact in
  every positive degree and at the augmentation. This is precisely the standard-cover
  {\v C}ech vanishing already established at the level of sections in
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.

  As this holds for every prime \(\mathfrak{p}\) — hence on every stalk — the
  augmented {\v C}ech complex is exact on \(X\); that is, the {\v C}ech nerve of
  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:cech_augmented_complex, lem:section_cech_homology_exact,
    lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology,
    lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
  We prove exactness of the augmented {\v C}ech complex of
  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) by showing that each homology
  object \(\mathcal{H}^p\) (\(p \geq 1\), together with the augmentation node) is the zero object. The
  argument works entirely with sections and sheafification, never with stalks.

  \emph{Step 1: reflect through the forgetful functor to abelian sheaves.} The forgetful functor from
  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to sheaves of abelian groups is faithful and additive, hence
  preserves zero morphisms; a faithful additive functor reflects the property of an object being zero.
  It therefore suffices to prove that the image of each homology object \(\mathcal{H}^p\) under this
  forgetful functor is zero in the category of abelian sheaves.

  \emph{Step 2: the homology sheaf is the sheafification of the presheaf homology.} By the
  sheafification-commutes-with-homology engine of
  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}, the degree-\(p\) homology of the complex of
  abelian sheaves is the sheafification of the degree-\(p\) homology of the underlying complex of
  \emph{presheaves}. The presheaf homology evaluated at an open \(V\) is the homology of the section
  complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})(V)\) --- the {\v C}ech cochain complex
  of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the cover restricted to \(V\) --- so the presheaf in question is
  \(V \mapsto \check{H}^p(V, \mathcal{F})\).

  \emph{Step 3: the presheaf homology vanishes locally on the affine basis.} This presheaf is not zero
  as a presheaf, but it vanishes on a basis. Over any basic affine \(D(g)\) small enough to sit inside
  a single cover element \(U_i\), the affine tilde isomorphism
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}) identifies \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g)}\) with \(\widetilde{M}\),
  and the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\widetilde{M}\) over the induced standard cover is exact in
  positive degrees: this is precisely Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover} (whose
  section-level exactness packaging is Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}). Since the basic
  affines contained in the cover elements form a basis cofinal among all opens, the canonical map
  \(0 \to (\text{presheaf homology})\) is a local isomorphism --- it is locally bijective. A map of
  sheaves is an isomorphism if and only if it is locally bijective, and sheafification carries a locally
  bijective map to an isomorphism; hence the sheafification of the presheaf homology is the zero sheaf.
  Combined with Step 2 this gives \(\mathcal{H}^p = 0\) for all \(p \geq 1\).

  \emph{Step 4: the augmentation node.} Separately, the augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to
  \mathcal{C}^0\) is the kernel inclusion: the degree-\(0\) homology of the augmented complex vanishes
  because over each basic affine the augmented section complex is exact at degree \(0\) (the same
  spanning-family argument that gives exactness in positive degrees produces exactness at the
  augmentation node), and sheafifying this local exactness as in Step 3 makes the augmentation node
  homology zero. Together with Steps 1--3, every homology object of the augmented complex is zero, so
  the complex is exact and the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of
  \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\).

  \emph{Remark.} The earlier stalk-at-prime argument --- exact iff exact on every stalk, with a
  ``one index is a unit'' contracting homotopy of the localized extended complex --- is not used here
  because it requires a stalk functor on sheaves of modules and an exact-iff-stalkwise-exact criterion
  that are not available in the ambient formalized library. Should the sections route's
  homology-versus-section bookkeeping prove unwieldy, a fallback remains: build the local ``insert the
  fixed index \(i\)'' contracting homotopy over each \(U_i\), combine it with exactness of restriction,
  and conclude by the same sheaf-local vanishing used in Step 3.
\end{proof}
